questions.question: Explain the difference between deep copy and shallow copy of a binary tree. Why is deep copy preferred in most applications?
keyPoints: Define shallow copy: copies references, shares node objects
Define deep copy: creates new nodes with separate memory
Deep copy ensures independence between original and copy
Modification in copy doesn't affect original
Essential for operations requiring data isolation

questions.question: Write an algorithm to perform level-order traversal of a binary tree. Explain the role of the queue data structure in this traversal.
keyPoints: Use queue data structure (FIFO property)
Initialize with root node
While queue not empty: dequeue, visit node, enqueue left and right children
Queue ensures level-by-level processing
Time complexity O(n), space depends on maximum queue size

questions.question: Given pre-order: A, B, D, E, C, F and in-order: D, B, E, A, C, F, reconstruct the binary tree and give its post-order traversal.
keyPoints: Use first element of pre-order as root (A)
Find root in in-order; elements left are left subtree, right are right subtree
Left subtree in-order: D, B, E; right subtree: C, F
Recursively construct left subtree (root B) and right subtree (root C)
Post-order: D, E, B, F, C, A

questions.question: What is the worst-case time complexity for searching an element in a binary tree? Under what conditions does this occur?
keyPoints: Worst-case time complexity: O(n) where n is number of nodes
Occurs in skewed/degenerate tree (linked list-like structure)
Every node has at most one child
May need to visit all nodes to find target
In balanced tree, search is O(log n)

questions.question: Explain the process of inserting a node in a binary tree to maintain it as a complete binary tree.
keyPoints: Find the first node with at least one empty child position
Use level-order traversal to locate this node
Attach new node as left child if left is empty, else as right child
This maintains complete tree property (all levels filled except possibly last)
Ensures minimum height for given number of nodes

questions.question: Define and differentiate between pre-order, in-order, and post-order traversals. For a binary tree with root R, left child L, and right child S (where L has children LL and LR), show all three traversal sequences with proper justification.
keyPoints: Pre-order: R-LL-LR-L-S (Root, Left, Right)
In-order: LL-LR-L-R-S (Left, Root, Right)
Post-order: LL-LR-L-S-R (Left, Right, Root)
Explain recursive nature of each
Show step-by-step traversal with specific example tree
Mention practical applications of each

questions.question: Write a complete recursive algorithm to create a deep copy of a binary tree. Analyze its time and space complexity, and explain why this approach ensures true independence between the original and copied trees.
keyPoints: Base case: if node is NULL, return NULL
Create new node with copied data
Recursively copy left subtree
Recursively copy right subtree
Assign copied subtrees to new node's children
Time complexity: O(n) - visit each node once
Space complexity: O(h) for recursion stack + O(n) for new tree storage
Deep copy allocates new memory for every node
Modifications to copy do not affect original

questions.question: A binary tree has n nodes. Prove that it has exactly n-1 edges. Also derive the relationship between the number of null pointers and the number of nodes in a binary tree.
keyPoints: Every node except root has exactly one parent edge
Total edges = number of non-null parent-child connections
Since each of n-1 nodes contributes exactly one edge, total = n-1
Each node has 2 child pointers, so 2n total pointer positions
n-1 of these are non-null (edges)
Therefore null pointers = 2n - (n-1) = n+1
Verify with examples: 1 node has 2 null, 2 nodes has 3 null
